questions.question: What does extending JpaRepository<User, Long> provide without writing any implementation?
options: Only findById and save — all other methods must be manually implemented
20+ CRUD methods including findById, findAll, save, delete, count, and existsById
All CRUD methods plus automatic REST endpoints at /api/users
Database schema generation and migration scripts

questions.question: What SQL does Spring Data JPA generate for the method findByRoleAndActiveTrue(String role)?
options: SELECT * FROM users WHERE role = ? AND active IS NOT NULL
SELECT * FROM users WHERE role = ?
SELECT * FROM users WHERE role = ? AND active = true
SELECT * FROM users WHERE role = ? OR active = true

questions.question: Why should service methods call userRepository.findById(id).orElseThrow() instead of .get()?
options: orElseThrow() is faster because it avoids boxing/unboxing
.get() requires importing java.util.NoSuchElementException explicitly
orElseThrow() lets you throw a domain-specific exception with a meaningful message instead of a generic NoSuchElementException
Spring Data JPA deprecated .get() in favor of orElseThrow()

questions.question: When is the @Transactional annotation required on a repository method?
options: Always — every repository method needs @Transactional to function
Only on @Query methods that modify data (UPDATE/DELETE) — they need @Transactional + @Modifying
Only on methods that return Optional<T>
Only when using native SQL queries with nativeQuery = true

questions.question: In JPQL (used in @Query), which syntax correctly references the User entity's email field?
options: WHERE users.email = :email (uses table name)
WHERE u.email = :email (uses entity alias defined in FROM clause)
WHERE User.email = :email (uses class name)
WHERE email = :email (no qualifier needed)
